articles.title: How Justin Ernest invested nearly $400M into hot startups without a traditional VC fund
articles.description: Instead of spending a year raising a formal venture fund, the Sabertooth VC founder used a captive network of LPs to invest startups like Anthropic, Anduril, and SpaceX.

articles.title: GM joins race to build batteries for AI data centers and the grid
articles.description: GM is developing an entirely new sodium-ion battery chemistry for use in everything from data centers to its own factories.

articles.title: Hey, Siri, here’s what I actually want from AI
articles.description: I'm desperate for a personal AI assistant, but do I really want to become the kind of person who can't function without the friendly robot voice in my phone?

articles.title: Anthropic’s Fable 5 can make weirdly fun video games with the click of a button
articles.description: Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 is going to be a big hit with the web's vibe coders.

articles.title: Can tech companies learn to love cheaper AI models?
articles.description: If those same AI workloads can be handled by cheaper models without affecting quality, it would mean a massive shift in the economics of AI.

articles.title: WWDC 2026: Everything announced on Siri AI, iOS 27, Apple Intelligence, and more
articles.description: Apple primarily made the case for an improved experience with its long-standing Siri assistant, which like most other announcements had a hefty helping of AI.

articles.title: CISA gives US federal agencies three days to fix a VPN bug under attack by a ransomware gang
articles.description: Check Point said hackers broke into dozens of organizations by exploiting a VPN bug in several of its products used across the government.

articles.title: Anthropic’s Claude Fable 5 is a version of Mythos the public can access today
articles.description: Anthropic is releasing Claude Fable 5, its first Mythos-class model available to the public. The model comes with guardrails that block responses in high-risk areas like cybersecurity and biology.

articles.title: Rivian starts deliveries of its all-important R2 SUV
articles.description: Founder and CEO RJ Scaringe has called it "maybe the most important thing we've launched to date."

articles.title: Apple’s foldable iPhone could be just around the corner
articles.description: The iOS 27 developer beta includes code that references the fold state and screen angle of a device.

articles.title: It’s not FAANG anymore. It’s MANGOS.
articles.description: With SpaceX, Anthropic, and OpenAI all eyeing massive public debuts, the tech industry may soon have a new class of corporate overlords — and a new acronym to match. Say goodbye to FAANG and hello to MANGOS.

articles.title: Apple says it may remove some apps from the App Store if they don’t attract users
articles.description: Apple may begin removing existing apps that it considers stale, low-value, or unable to attract users.

articles.title: iOS 27 features we didn’t see onstage
articles.description: While iOS 27 isn't bringing major changes, it's still got many small features that could improve everyday usage for iPhone users.

articles.title: Apple brings streaming-style subscription bundles to the App Store
articles.description: Apple is expanding its App Bundles feature to allow developers to partner with one another on discounted subscription packages.

articles.title: Apple’s App Store rolls out personalized recommendations
articles.description: Apple will now recommend apps based on your downloads and behavior.

articles.title: Sandstone raises $30M to bring AI to in-house legal teams
articles.description: Sandstone's Series A comes just six months after a Sequoia-led seed round.

articles.title: Lovable says it has hit $500M in annualized revenue, with 1 million new projects a week
articles.description: Lovable says it has now surpassed $500 million in annualized run-rate revenue and its users are building businesses and replacing internal software.

articles.title: How an e-scooter founder raised $5 million to build space data centers
articles.description: Orbital founder Euwyn Poon built 250,000 scooters at Spin. Now he wants to launch 10,000 space data centers.

articles.title: Evotrex raises $30M to build the RV that doesn’t need a charging station
articles.description: The startup is one of many entering the RV space, but it's banking on a hybrid power system that can go far beyond campsites.

articles.title: Zepto’s IPO filing reveals fast growth, bigger losses, and a valuation question nobody’s answered yet
articles.description: Zepto's advertising revenue jumped 151%, outpacing the company's 104% growth in operating revenue.

articles.title: Starlink charges $10 monthly hardware fee in move away from one-time purchases
articles.description: Starlink, SpaceX's top moneymaker, also raised service prices by $5 to $10.

articles.title: Locked in heated rivalry with researcher, Microsoft fixes 0-day they disclosed
articles.description: A separate zero-day also disclosed by Nightmare Eclipse appears to be patched as well.

articles.title: Three key vital signs make up the "urban pulse" of a city
articles.description: Cities are dynamic, not static grids, and urbanization is a "spiky," cyclical, and asynchronous process.

articles.title: Commonwealth Fusion makes the physics case for its 400 MW reactor
articles.description: Five peer-reviewed papers update the design and model its expected output.

articles.title: Paramount accuses Netflix of "scorched-earth campaign" against WBD merger
articles.description: Netflix's response: "Absurd."

articles.title: Anthropic says these topics are too dangerous to let its Fable 5 model talk about
articles.description: New frontier model refuses cybersecurity, biology, and chemistry queries.

articles.title: Google announces Gemini 3.5 Live Translate for instant voice-to-voice translation
articles.description: Voice translations preserve speaker's tone, pacing, pitch—with SynthID watermarks for security.

articles.title: NASA assigns crew for Artemis III, sets aggressive timeline for flying it
articles.description: "Artemis III will be an extraordinary demonstration of what is possible."

articles.title: Screwworms in US: Human risk is low—but they can burrow through your skull
articles.description: The chances are low, but not zero.

articles.title: One day after discovery, Meta pulls facial recognition code from its smart glasses
articles.description: Meta won't say why or whether it's coming back.

articles.title: Drone boat picked up downed US Army helicopter pilots—a first for sea rescues
articles.description: US Navy’s Task Force 59 achieved the drone rescue at sea near Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: High-severity vulnerability in Linux caused by a single faulty character
articles.description: Use-after-free bug can be exploited to evade sandbox defenses.

articles.title: Gold isn’t inert, it just has bodyguards protecting it
articles.description: Individual gold atoms move around to form oxidation-proof structures.

articles.title: Here's Audi's next Q7 SUV and US-only SQ7, now with an RS V8
articles.description: At night, the car projects its turn signals onto the road to alert other road users.

articles.title: Apple says its AI is still private, even when it's running on Google's servers
articles.description: Some models run in Google's cloud, but without giving Google any kind of access.

articles.title: First Drive: The 2027 Rivian R2 entirely changes the EV game
articles.description: Rivian's second EV is the sub-$60,000 R2, and it was worth the wait.

articles.title: FCC lifts looming deadline for Amazon Leo satellite broadband constellation
articles.description: The waiver "serves the public interest by promoting a second large satellite broadband constellation."

articles.title: Tests suggest Russian satellites can jam GPS on a continental scale
articles.description: Mystery of GPS interference across Europe raises questions about Russian motives.

articles.title: macOS 27 requires Apple Silicon, as Apple draws down the Intel Mac era
articles.description: You'll need an M1 or better to run the next release of macOS.

articles.title: iOS 27 and iPadOS 27 don't drop support for any iPhones—and just a few iPads
articles.description: This promises to be a solid release for aging iPhones.

tech.summary:
今日科技动态摘要

1. **非传统投资与造车新动向**：贾斯汀·欧内斯特以近4亿美元投资热门初创公司，未依赖传统风投基金；通用汽车加入为AI数据中心和电网建设电池的竞争行列。
2. **AI产品与模型发展**：Anthropic推出能一键生成趣味视频游戏的Fable 5模型，并发布公众可访问的Claude Fable 5版本；苹果在WWDC 2026上公布Siri AI、iOS 27及Apple Intelligence等更新，同时探讨科技公司对更廉价AI模型的接受度。
3. **苹果生态与硬件更新**：苹果可能即将推出折叠屏iPhone，iOS 27新增未在发布会提及的功能，App Store推出流媒体式订阅套餐、个性化推荐，并警告将移除长期无用户吸引力的应用。
4. **网络安全与汽车交付**：美国网络安全与基础设施安全局（CISA）要求联邦机构三天内修复遭勒索软件攻击的VPN漏洞；Rivian开始交付关键车型R2 SUV。
5. **行业格局变化**：FAANG（脸书、苹果、亚马逊、奈飞、谷歌）被重新定义为MANGOS（Meta、苹果、奈飞、谷歌、微软、特斯拉等），反映科技巨头格局的演变。

**今日最值得关注的发展**：苹果在WWDC 2026上全面升级AI与生态服务，同时Anthropic推出可一键生成游戏的Fable 5模型，标志AI应用从工具型向创意娱乐型快速拓展。

ai.summary:
今日AI动态摘要

1. **语音代理与多语言能力**：Hugging Face 发布了一项针对语音代理处理双语用户（代码切换语音）的基准测试，评估前沿自动语音识别（ASR）模型的表现。
2. **开发者工具与模型更新**：Cohere 推出首款面向开发者的模型“North Mini Code”；Hugging Face 介绍了将 GitHub CI 迁移至其 Jobs 平台的方法，并设计了 Agent 优化的 hf CLI 工具；OpenAI 展示了 Codex 在 Nextdoor 和 Notion 中的实际应用。
3. **开源与强化学习新进展**：开源社区支持 OpenEnv 用于智能体强化学习（Agentic RL）；Hugging Face 发布 Nemotron 3.5 内容安全模型，提供可定制的多模态安全方案；同时探索了超越聊天机器人的直接偏好优化（DPO）方法。
4. **AI 应用与搜索创新**：Google AI 博客宣布 2026 年 5 月最新 AI 新闻，并介绍 Google 搜索在二手购物中的 5 种提升体验方式；Hugging Face 展示如何通过链接两个 Spaces 构建 3D 巴黎画廊，以及为 Reachy Mini 机器人添加 MCP 工具。
5. **OpenAI 战略与合规进展**：OpenAI 向 SEC 秘密提交了 S-1 草案，并发布“惠及所有人”的计划，强调其构建通用受益 AI 的愿景。

今日最值得关注的发展是 **开源社区与各大厂商在智能体（Agent）与强化学习方向加速布局**，从语音代理、开发者工具到机器人控制，AI 正从单一模型走向可交互、可执行的智能体生态。
